name: PagerDuty
description: Incident management and on-call orchestration platform. Yields incident records with immutable timelines, post-incident reviews, on-call schedule coverage, escalation policies and an account audit trail as evidence for incident response and operational resilience requirements.

steps.action: Navigate to Incidents in the web app's top navigation, filter by status Resolved and set the date range to cover the collection period
Open a sample of resolved incidents and capture each incident's timeline (screenshot or print to PDF) showing trigger, notification, acknowledgement, any escalations and resolution
steps.audit_note: Incident log entries are timestamped by PagerDuty and cannot be edited, making the timeline strong evidence of response times

steps.action: For major incidents, export the Post-Incident Review report (formerly Postmortems — legacy Postmortem reports were converted to Post-Incident Review opportunities in January 2026) showing the curated timeline, analysis and follow-up actions
Capture on-call coverage: navigate to People > On-Call Schedules, open each in-scope schedule and screenshot the rota for the period, confirming there are no coverage gaps
steps.audit_note: Capture the full period including handovers — gaps in the rota undermine incident response assurance

steps.action: Capture escalation configuration: navigate to People > Escalation Policies and screenshot each in-scope policy showing escalation levels, targets and timeouts
Upload the incident exports, review reports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Incident notes may contain sensitive system details or personal data — review before uploading

L2.title: PagerDuty Recurring Exports via Insights and Calendar Feeds

steps.action: Note: PagerDuty has no built-in scheduled email report delivery. The closest no-code mechanisms are Insights CSV exports on a diarised cadence and continuously updating calendar feeds for on-call schedules
steps.audit_note: Because exports are user-triggered, diarise the task and record completion to evidence the cadence

steps.action: Navigate to Analytics > Insights > Incident Activity, set the date range to the collection period and use Export CSV to download incident metrics for the period
steps.permissions_required: Available on Professional, Business, Digital Operations (legacy) and Enterprise for Incident Management plans
steps.audit_note: Insights data is updated on a daily pipeline cycle (typically within 24 hours), so export after the period has fully closed

steps.action: Subscribe an evidence calendar to each in-scope on-call schedule's WebCal/iCal export feed (People > On-Call Schedules > select schedule > Export) so rota coverage is captured continuously without manual screenshots
steps.security_note: The calendar feed URL embeds an access token — share it only with the evidence mailbox/calendar, and regenerate it if exposed

steps.action: Each cycle, review the Audit Trail report for key users (People > Users > select user > View Audit Trail) to evidence configuration changes to schedules and escalation policies
steps.permissions_required: Admin base role or Account Owner

steps.action: File the CSV exports and audit trail extracts in the evidence repository under a consistent naming convention, and record the diarised task as complete
steps.audit_note: The completed-task record plus dated exports together evidence a repeatable, scheduled process

steps.action: Create a read-only REST API key: navigate to Integrations > API Access Keys, select Create New API Key, enter a description and tick 'Read-only API Key' to restrict it to GET calls
steps.permissions_required: Admin base role or Account Owner
steps.security_note: The key is displayed in full only once — store it in a secrets manager immediately and rotate it on a schedule. Never use a personal user token for automation
steps.audit_note: API key creation is captured in the account audit trail

steps.action: Collect incident evidence via GET https://api.pagerduty.com/incidents?since={start}&until={end}&statuses[]=resolved (Authorization: Token token={key}), then GET https://api.pagerduty.com/incidents/{id}/log_entries for each incident's full timeline
steps.audit_note: Log entries are the immutable record of who was notified, when they acknowledged and how the incident was resolved

steps.action: Collect on-call coverage and configuration via GET https://api.pagerduty.com/oncalls (point-in-time coverage), GET https://api.pagerduty.com/schedules and GET https://api.pagerduty.com/escalation_policies
steps.audit_note: Daily /oncalls snapshots build a continuous coverage record that a single screenshot cannot provide

steps.action: Collect the account audit trail via GET https://api.pagerduty.com/audit/records with cursor-based pagination, capturing configuration changes to services, schedules and escalation policies
steps.permissions_required: REST API key (account-level); feature availability depends on pricing plan
steps.audit_note: Pull the audit trail at least monthly so records are preserved beyond PagerDuty's retention window

steps.action: Collect aggregated response metrics (MTTA/MTTR, incident counts) via POST https://api.pagerduty.com/analytics/metrics/incidents/all with a JSON filter body for the period and in-scope teams/services
steps.audit_note: Analytics data is refreshed once a day and is not real time — run the export after the period closes

steps.action: Schedule the collection daily via cron or CI/CD, write date-stamped raw JSON to the evidence repository, implement error handling with alerting on failed runs, and validate daily incident counts against a rolling baseline
steps.security_note: Inject the API key from the secrets manager at runtime; never commit it to source control
steps.audit_note: Volume validation and failure alerts evidence collection completeness

L4.title: PagerDuty Managed Evidence Pipeline with Webhooks and Metrics

steps.action: Configure v3 webhooks: navigate to Integrations > Generic Webhooks (v3), create a subscription for incident lifecycle events (triggered, acknowledged, escalated, reassigned, resolved) delivering to your evidence platform or SIEM
steps.permissions_required: Admin base role or Account Owner (Team Managers can manage subscriptions scoped to their teams via the Service Details page)
steps.security_note: Use an HTTPS endpoint with TLS 1.2+ and verify the X-PagerDuty-Signature header on every delivery before ingesting

steps.action: Implement daily reconciliation: compare webhook-received incident events against GET https://api.pagerduty.com/incidents for the same window and alert on any incident present in one source but not the other
steps.audit_note: Webhook delivery is at-least-once but not guaranteed; REST reconciliation is what makes the pipeline evidentially complete

steps.action: Add continuous coverage monitoring: poll GET https://api.pagerduty.com/oncalls on a short interval and raise an alert when any in-scope escalation policy level has no on-call responder
steps.audit_note: Automated gap detection turns the on-call rota from static evidence into a monitored control

steps.action: Run automated quality checks on ingested events — schema validation, duplicate suppression on event IDs, and time-series gap detection per service
Build a collection health dashboard (events/day, webhook delivery latency, reconciliation mismatches, on-call coverage %) and alert when metrics breach thresholds
steps.audit_note: The dashboard is itself continuous evidence that the collection process is managed and monitored

steps.action: Generate an automated quarterly collection health report covering completeness, reconciliation results, coverage gaps and SLA breaches, and file it in the evidence repository
